name: Raspberry Laced Vanilla Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Cups Margarine Or Butter (3 Sticks), softened
recipeInstructions:
About 3 hours before serving or early in the day:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour three 9-inch round cake pans.
In a large bowl, with mixer on low speed, beat margarine or butter and sugar just until blended. Increase speed to high; beat until light and fluffy, scraping bowl often with rubber spatula. Reduce speed to low; add flour, milk, baking powder, vanilla extract, salt, baking soda, and eggs; beat until well mixed, constantly scraping bowl.
Increase speed to high; beat 2 minutes, occasionally scraping bowl.
Spoon batter into pans, spreading evenly. Bake 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center of the cakes comes out clean. Cool in pans on wire racks for 10 minutes. With a spatula, loosen cake layers from edges of pans; invert onto wire racks to cool completely.
Prepare Raspberry Butter Cream Frosting:
In a large bowl, with mixer on low speed, beat 3 cups confectioners' sugar and 2 cups margarine or butter (4 sticks) softened until just mixed. Increase speed to high; beat for 10 minutes or until light and fluffy, scraping bowl often with rubber spatula. Reduce speed to medium, gradually beat in 1/4 cup raspberry flavor liquid and 1 tsp vanilla extract until smooth, occasionally scraping bowl with rubber spatula.
Spoon 2 cups frosting into decorating bag with small star tube; set aside.
With a serrated knife, cut each cake layer horizontally in half. Place the bottom half of one layer, cut side up, on a cake plate; spread with 1/3 cup raspberry preserves. Top with the top half of the cake layer; spread with about 1/2 cup butter cream frosting. Repeat layering, ending with a cake layer.
With a metal spatula, spread remaining butter cream frosting on sides and top of cake. Use frosting in decorating bag to pipe lattice top and pretty border on cake. Refrigerate cake if not serving right away. Makes 24 servings.
